description.value: The data format relevant to genomics. These formats and relevant codes were pulled from [Integrative Genomics Viewer Documentation](https://software.broadinstitute.org/software/igv/FileFormats) by Broad Institute.

definition.value: Binary Alignment/Map format for storing read alignments against reference sequences.
Browser Extensible Data format for representing genomic regions and associated annotations.
Paired-End BED format for representing pairwise genomic interactions.
BED Graph format for representing genomic signals as continuous-valued data.
Binary indexed BED format for efficiently storing large amounts of genomic region data.
Binary indexed Wig format for efficiently storing large amounts of continuous-valued genomic data.
File format used by the Birdsuite suite of software for SNP genotyping and copy number analysis.
BED format variant for representing broad peaks in ChIP-Seq data.
Copy number data format output by Circular Binary Segmentation analysis.
Profiles of chemical reactivity for RNA structure analysis.
File listing chromosome names and their sizes.
Copy number data format.
User-defined or proprietary file formats for genomic data.
Chromosome cytogenetic band locations and characteristics.
Format for representing sequences of nucleic acids or proteins using single letter codes.
Gene Cluster Text format for storing gene expression data.
Compressed Reference-Aligned Map format for storing read alignments more compactly than BAM.
Format for storing gene predictions with exon and CDS information.
General Feature Format / Gene Transfer Format for storing genomic features and annotations.
Genomic Identification of Significant Targets in Cancer output format for copy number analysis.
Compact file format for storing read alignments, variations, and base quality information.
Genome-Wide Association Study format for storing association results.
Integrative Genomics Viewer session or display format.
Loss of Heterozygosity data format.
Multiple Alignment Format for storing aligned sequences.
Mutation Annotation Format for storing somatic mutation data.
BAM file containing read alignments from multiple samples or lanes merged together.
Mutation data format.
BED format variant for representing narrow peaks in ChIP-Seq data.
Pattern Space Layout format for storing sequence alignments.
Resolution data format.
Formats for representing RNA secondary structure information.
Sequence Alignment/Map format for storing read alignments, the uncompressed version of BAM.
File containing sample information and attributes.
Segmented data format for storing copy number or other segmented genomic data.
Tiled Data Format for efficient storage and display of large genomic datasets.
UCSC Genome Browser track line header defining display properties for genomic data.
Type line header for defining genomic data track properties.
Variant Call Format for storing variant information including SNPs, indels, and structural variations.
Wiggle Track format for storing continuous-valued genomic data.
